text: Clean code is easier to read and understand. One important habit is to use clear naming conventions. Variables and functions should have names that describe their purpose, like `calculateTotalPrice` instead of `ctp`. This makes the code self-explanatory. Functions should do one thing only. A short function with a single task is much easier to test and reuse. Finally, good code is maintainable. When you or another developer return to the code after months, clear structure and naming help you quickly fix bugs or add new features. Following these simple rules saves time and reduces frustration.
